copy.tagline: Buy the bottom. Own the recovery.
copy.thesis: San Antonio's 1978 workforce housing acquired mid-cycle while vacancy peaks. Renovate 50 of 64 units while rents are soft; by Year 3 the 80% collapse in 2024 apartment starts translates into occupancy recovery. Exit stabilized, supply-constrained NOI in Year 7 — before the next wave can form.

marketFindings.headline: Vacancy at 15.7% — highest of the 50 largest US apartment markets
marketFindings.body: San Antonio ranked #1 for apartment vacancies in Q1 2026, driven by the 2023–2025 delivery wave. Rents are down 3.3% YoY. The disruption is real and baked into our Year 1–2 underwriting.

sources.title: San Antonio has highest apartment vacancy rates in US — CultureMap

marketFindings.headline: 2026 deliveries fall 63% — slowest construction pace since 2011
marketFindings.body: Only ~2,600 units deliver in 2026 vs. 7,000+ in 2025. Apartment starts collapsed 80% in 2024. At current rents and construction costs almost no new projects pencil, making the supply constraint structural rather than cyclical.

sources.title: Multifamily construction pipeline thinning in San Antonio — Northmarq
San Antonio multifamily construction to slow further in 2026 — Northmarq

marketFindings.headline: San Antonio leads Texas employment growth; 4th fastest-growing US city
marketFindings.body: Payrolls grew an annualized 2.2% YTD through April 2025, led by construction, healthcare, and government. The metro added 23,900 residents in 2024 — sustaining demand for workforce housing through the supply overhang.

sources.title: San Antonio will lead employment growth in Texas — Texas Public Radio
San Antonio ranks as nation's 4th fastest-growing city — SA Current

marketFindings.headline: Cap rates widened to ~6.0% — buying 27% below the Q1 2026 median price-per-unit
marketFindings.body: Broader San Antonio multifamily cap rates averaged 6.0% in Q1 2026 (up 50bps from 2024); C-class averages ~5.4%. The Q1 2026 median sale price was $132,600/unit. We enter at $96,875/unit, a 27% discount, reflecting renovation need and current softness.

sources.title: 2025 Multifamily Recap & 2026 Trends in Texas — SWBC
San Antonio Multifamily Market Report — Yardi Matrix

marketFindings.headline: Distressed sales increasing as income falls short of debt thresholds
marketFindings.body: Class C properties were 45% of Q1 2026 transactions, many driven by forced sales. We underwrite Year 1 at 78% effective occupancy and zero rent growth — consistent with observed stressed pricing — and hold 18 months of interest reserves at close.

sources.title: San Antonio Multifamily Market Outlook 2026 — Neiboroughly
Multifamily Cap Rates Stabilize as Investor Sentiment Strengthens — Multi-Housing News
